title: Put: A European put option allows the holder to sell the...
summary: Calculate the intrinsic value of a put option from strike price and stock price.
prompt: A European put option allows the holder to sell the underlying asset at the strike price only at expiration. If the current stock price is $40 and the strike price is $35, what is the intrinsic value?

options.text: The intrinsic value of a put option is max(0, Strike Price - Stock Price) =

solution: The intrinsic value of a put option is max(0, Strike Price - Stock Price) = max(0, 35 - 40) = $0. [[24]]
